_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C12: 96 group dimension updates (25 confirmed, 38 corrected, 33 newly assigned); 5 Session B findings; 1 Session D pointer

record.finding: The new covenant transformation group (3272-001: diatithēmi — the covenantal act writing divine law on the inner mind and heart) appears in the abomination registry. This places the new covenant's inner transformation as the divine answer to abomination and inner defilement. Session B should examine whether the abomination cluster's verse evidence shows the new covenant specifically as the remedy for abomination — and whether this pattern recurs across the cluster.

operations.description: Session B pointer — abomination: new covenant transformation as divine answer to abomination

record.finding: Group 779-001 (asōtia — debauchery) is explicitly contrasted with Spirit-filling in Eph 5:18: 'do not be drunk with wine, in which is debauchery, but be filled with the Spirit.' This creates a fundamental inner-being polarity: disordered inner filling (debauchery) vs. divine inner filling (Spirit). Session B should map this antithesis as a structural inner-being pair and examine whether it reflects a broader pattern in the cluster between defilement and the Spirit's filling/cleansing.

operations.description: Session B pointer — debauchery: debauchery/Spirit-filling as structural inner-being antithesis

record.finding: Group 5574-001 (akathartos — unclean spirit) presents the unclean spirit as an inner occupant whose presence produces inner captivity and whose expulsion by Christ restores inner freedom. Session B should examine this as a model of inner-being occupation and liberation — the inner person as the site of spiritual occupancy — and how this relates to the programme's understanding of the inner being's relationship to spiritual forces.

operations.description: Session B pointer — impurity: unclean spirit as inner occupant; Christ's expulsion as inner liberation

record.finding: The slander registry contains three foot-related groups (6253-001: moral life direction; 6253-002: relational submission/devotion; 6256-001: teaching to walk) whose connection to slander is via the XREF lexical path rather than conceptual affinity. Session B should assess whether these groups contribute meaningfully to the slander analysis or whether they represent peripheral inner-being vocabulary that should be noted as structurally incidental.

operations.description: Session B pointer — slander: foot-related groups structurally peripheral to slander concept

record.finding: All seven whoredom groups are dominated by spiritual/covenantal whoredom — Israel's heart-departure toward foreign gods. The literal sexual dimension appears in only one group (1221-004). Session B should examine how the spiritual whoredom metaphor relates to the literal sexual meaning as inner-being phenomena, and whether covenantal faithfulness is the primary inner-being register of this vocabulary in Scripture.

operations.description: Session B pointer — whoredom: spiritual/covenantal whoredom dominates; literal dimension marginal

record.description: The new covenant transformation group (3272-001) appears in the abomination registry and salvation vocabulary groups appear in the debauchery registry (Reg 39) — both placing redemptive vocabulary within moral-failure/defilement registries through the XREF mechanism. Session D should examine whether this is a programme-wide pattern: are there other clusters where redemptive/salvation vocabulary appears in moral-failure registries via XREF, and what does this reveal about the inner-being logic connecting defilement and redemption in Scripture?
